frameworks.name: risk vs ambiguity (decision under uncertainty)
frameworks.one_line: distinguishes choices with known vs unknown outcome probabilities

frameworks.name: lesion-behavior mapping
frameworks.one_line: infers regional function from focal lesion effects on choice

frameworks.name: computational modeling of choice
frameworks.one_line: parameterizes risk and ambiguity attitudes from behavior

frameworks.name: arousal/autonomic signaling of value
frameworks.one_line: tests whether peripheral arousal couples to uncertain choice

core_question: Which prefrontal and amygdala regions are functionally required for decisions under risk versus ambiguity?
key_assumptions: risk and ambiguity are dissociable psychological constructs
focal lesions reveal necessary regional contributions
manipulations: lesion site (mPFC vs lPFC vs amygdala vs controls)
uncertainty type (risk with known probabilities vs ambiguity with unknown probabilities)
key_findings: lPFC lesions increased both risk and ambiguity seeking
lPFC lesions disrupted the arousal-choice relationship
mPFC and amygdala lesions did not impair risk processing
mPFC showed only a tenuous link to ambiguity
interpretations: lPFC operates as a unitary system processing the aversive nature of uncertainty
risk and ambiguity share an lPFC-based substrate rather than separable mPFC/amygdala substrates
